chapter_goal: Make curvature, signed curvature, osculating circles, torsion, and Frenet-Serret frames visible and testable.

library_routing_table.why: 2D incidence, orientation, and local-circle constructions need durable annotated figures.
the key formulas simplify exactly before numerical plotting.
torsion and binormal rotation are spatial and benefit from rotation.
the construction is an integral recipe from signed curvature to a curve.

source_span_read.book: Andrew Pressley, Elementary Differential Geometry, Second Edition
source_span_read.title: How much does a curve curve?

visual_sequence.inspection_target: the tangent-line deviation scales quadratically and circle curvature stays fixed under reparameterization
sign changes in curvature match clockwise/counterclockwise tangent motion
visual_sequence.validation: d phi/ds residual and closed-curve turning number

visual_sequence.inspection_target: centers of curvature trace the evolute and nearby three-point circles converge
pitch changes torsion while the plane-circle case has zero torsion
T, N, B remain orthonormal and right-handed
binormal movement out of the osculating plane
visual_sequence.validation: same helix residual payload as the static plot

visual_sequence.inspection_target: integrating kappa_s controls tangent angle first, position second
